varieties.name: H2 (Hinde) 🏆
varieties.origin: Colliston, Gold Coast hinterland, Queensland, c.1920s. Parent tree still survives. Named after Hinde family property.
varieties.traits: Medium-large tree, vigorous growth. Dense canopy. Cream-white flowers. Smooth-margined leaves in whorls of 3.
varieties.yield_notes: Good consistent yields. Reliable producer.
varieties.availability: Widely available as rootstock from Queensland nurseries. 90% of Australian grafted trees use H2 rootstock. Historical significance as one of only two Australian varieties endorsed 1960s-1980s.

varieties.name: Walter Hill Tree (Brisbane Botanic Gardens) 🏆
varieties.origin: Hawaii, early 20th century (1920s). Hawaiian Agricultural Experiment Station selection. From Queensland genetic base.
varieties.traits: Upright tree with dense canopy. Better wind resistance than other varieties. Adaptable to wide range of climates. Yellow-hued blossoms September flowering.
varieties.yield_notes: Early season cropping. Reliable yields. 3.2-3.9 fruits per raceme.
varieties.availability: Unique heritage specimen. Not commercially propagated but accessible for viewing humid subtropical climates Botanic Gardens rainforest section.

varieties.name: A4
varieties.origin: Australia, Hidden Valley selection. Modern commercial hybrid.
varieties.traits: Medium-sized tree with open canopy. Vigorous and precocious. Moderate growth. Early bearing year 4-5. Open canopy but brittle viny branches prone to wind damage.
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate at 3-4 t/ha. Alternate bearing tendency. Precocious - early commercial yields year 3. Well suited to high density planting.
varieties.availability: Widely available Queensland nurseries. Popular commercial variety. Premium price $40-60 per tree. Daley's, Southern Cross, regional suppliers.

varieties.name: A16
varieties.traits: Upright growth but brittle branches. Aggressive regrowth post-pruning. Yellow-hued flowers extending into October.
varieties.yield_notes: Early bearing year 4-5, after Beaumont and A4. Higher yields than A4, up to 3 t/ha. Mid-season harvest.
varieties.availability: Queensland nurseries. Industry standard variety. Used in regional variety trials throughout Queensland.

varieties.name: A38
varieties.traits: Medium-sized tree. Good structure.
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable yields.

varieties.name: A203
varieties.traits: Modern commercial variety. Good tree structure.
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields. Modern high-performance cultivar.
varieties.availability: Licensed nurseries only. RedSun Hortitech accredited to propagate. South African and Australian commercial plantings.

varieties.name: A268
varieties.traits: Medium tree size. Good canopy structure.
varieties.yield_notes: Excellent yields. Industry standard comparison variety.

varieties.name: Beaumont (695)
varieties.origin: Discovered by Dr. J.H. Beaumont, University of Hawaii. Most widely planted cultivar South Africa. Industry standard.
varieties.traits: Upright growth habit, space-efficient. Bright pink flowers on long racemes. Vibrant reddish new leaves. Dense canopy. Impressive grape-like nut clusters can break branchlets. Nuts don't drop when ripe - stick-tight.
varieties.yield_notes: Quickest to bearing - useful crop by year 4, improving thereafter. Crops prodigiously when well-pollinated. 18kg per tree by year 8. High yield potential. Alternate bearing in some locations. Late season harvest.
varieties.availability: Widely available Queensland nurseries. Daley's, Southern Cross, regional suppliers. Most popular home garden variety. Common rootstock - H2 and 695 are industry standard rootstocks globally. Price $30-50 per tree. Available both grafted trees and rooted clonal cuttings.

varieties.name: Daddow
varieties.traits: Vigorous dense spreading tree. Smooth-leaved. Good canopy density.
varieties.yield_notes: Best all-round performer over six different trial sites throughout QLD and NSW. Consistent reliable production. Proven across diverse conditions.

varieties.name: Own Choice
varieties.traits: Good tree structure. Standard growth pattern.
varieties.yield_notes: Good pollinator for Beaumont. Crops moderately well. May be self-fertile. Nuts must be picked when ripe (don't drop).

varieties.name: Own Venture
varieties.traits: Good commercial growth characteristics.
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable commercial yields.

varieties.name: MiniMaca (Dwarf Macadamia)
varieties.origin: Northern NSW discovery. Rare unique miniature selection. Plant Breeders Rights protected.
varieties.traits: Unique miniature features. Grows to half normal size. Stunning pink new growth. Columnar growth habit. Can be maintained as hedge with regular pruning. Semi-dwarfing, compact tree. Slow growing. Wind tolerant.
varieties.yield_notes: High yielder for its size. Excellent nut quality. Precocious bearer.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery. Rare collector's variety. Plant Breeders Rights - propagation prohibited. Premium specialty selection. Ideal for Australian bushfood collectors.

varieties.name: Pink Flowering (Ornamental)
varieties.traits: Stunning pink racemes of flowers in Spring. Attractive pink flowers and maroon new growth. Popular for ornamental colorful features. Propagation prohibited (trademarked).
varieties.yield_notes: Produces well after 4th year. Nuts fall when ripe from Autumn through Winter.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery and specialty nurseries. Trademarked variety. Ornamental macadamia selection. Warm temperate to subtropical zones.

varieties.name: Beaumont (695)
varieties.origin: Discovered Dr. J.H. Beaumont, University of Hawaii. Widely planted globally.
varieties.traits: Upright space-efficient growth. Pink flowers, reddish new leaves. Quick to bearing. Nuts don't drop (stick-tight).
varieties.yield_notes: Quickest bearing (year 4). Prodigious yields when pollinated. 18kg by year 8. High yield potential. Drought tolerant.

varieties.name: A4
varieties.traits: Open canopy, vigorous precocious growth. Brittle branches.
varieties.yield_notes: Precocious - early commercial yields year 3. Moderate 3-4 t/ha. High density suitable.

varieties.name: A16
varieties.traits: Upright growth, brittle branches. Aggressive regrowth.
varieties.yield_notes: Higher than A4, up to 3 t/ha. Early bearing year 4-5. Mid-season.

varieties.name: Kakea (508/HAES 508)
varieties.traits: Good tree structure. Proven Hawaiian cultivar.
varieties.yield_notes: Higher yield than 246 (Keauhou). Good commercial production.
varieties.availability: Hawaiian variety available through international suppliers. May be sourced through agricultural research contacts India. Tested Bangalore conditions successfully.

varieties.name: Keaau (660/HAES 660)
varieties.origin: Hawaii. Named after Keaau region Hawaii.
varieties.traits: Upright growth pattern. Favored over spreading types. Good structure.
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding. Good commercial production.
varieties.availability: Hawaiian variety. May be available through specialized Indian importers or agricultural research institutions. Tested in Indian conditions.

varieties.name: Nelmak 2 (Nelspruit Macadamia 2)
varieties.origin: South Africa, Nelspruit ARC 1973. Nelspruit Agricultural Research Council selection. Named Nelspruit Makadamia in Afrikaans.
varieties.traits: Vigorous prolific grower. Fragrant pink flowers. Nuts hang in clusters. Beautiful ornamental.

varieties.name: Macadamia integrifolia Seedling 🌱
varieties.origin: 🌏 INDIGENOUS - Native Queensland/NSW Australia. Wild genetic diversity.
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree 10-20m tall. Smooth-margined mature leaves (juvenile leaves serrated). Whorls of 3 leaves. Cream-white flowers. Green new growth. Smooth spherical shells.
varieties.yield_notes: Variable. Takes 10-12 years seed to production (vs 4-6 years grafted). Some produce well, others minimal. Unpredictable.

varieties.name: Macadamia tetraphylla Seedling 🌱
varieties.origin: 🌏 INDIGENOUS - Native Gold Coast to Northern NSW Australia. Bush Nut. Wild genetic diversity.
varieties.traits: Evergreen tree up to 20m. Four spiny serrated leaves per whorl (vs 3 for integrifolia). Pink flowers. Pink/maroon new growth. Rough pebbled shells. Better cold tolerance than integrifolia.
varieties.yield_notes: Variable. 10-12 years seed to production. Some productive, others minimal. Unpredictable yields.
varieties.availability: Some tropical monsoon climates nurseries may stock. Greens of tropical monsoon climates region. Less common than integrifolia seedlings. Better suited cooler Indian hill stations (Kodaikanal, Munnar highlands). Lower cost option for experimentation.
